analysis.summary: Growstuff is a Ruby on Rails web application for food gardeners that crowdsources planting, growing, and harvesting data to create an open dataset. It implements a monolithic architecture with member-driven content, garden management, crop tracking, and photo sharing capabilities. The system integrates with external APIs like Flickr and provides both web UI and JSON API endpoints for accessing agricultural data.
capabilities: Manages member authentication and profiles with Devise gem (app/models/member.rb, config/initializers/devise.rb)
Tracks crop plantings with planting dates, garden locations, and status tracking (app/models/planting.rb, app/controllers/plantings_controller.rb)
Records harvest data with quantities, plant parts, and harvest dates (app/models/harvest.rb, app/controllers/harvests_controller.rb)
Manages garden spaces with location data, types, and member collaboration (app/models/garden.rb, app/controllers/gardens_controller.rb)
Maintains comprehensive crop database with scientific names, companions, and growing metadata (app/models/crop.rb, app/controllers/crops_controller.rb)
Processes seed tracking with variety information and trading capabilities (app/models/seed.rb, app/controllers/seeds_controller.rb)
Handles photo uploads and associations with crops, plantings, and harvests (app/models/photo.rb, app/controllers/photos_controller.rb)
Implements activity planning and tracking with due dates and garden assignments (app/models/activity.rb, app/controllers/activities_controller.rb)
Provides JSON:API v1 endpoints for crops, gardens, plantings, harvests, seeds, and activities (app/controllers/api/v1/*, swagger/v1/swagger.json)
Manages forum discussions and posts with commenting system (app/models/post.rb, app/controllers/posts_controller.rb)
Handles member-to-member messaging with Mailboxer gem integration (app/controllers/conversations_controller.rb)
Implements role-based authorization with CanCanCan for admin functions (app/models/ability.rb, app/controllers/admin/)
Tracks crop companions and planting relationships (app/models/crop_companion.rb, app/controllers/admin/crop_companions_controller.rb)
Integrates with Flickr API for photo management using FlickRaw gem (app/models/concerns/member_flickr.rb, Gemfile)
Provides data export in CSV format with CSVShaper gem (app/views/activities/index.csv.shaper)
Implements timeline visualization for garden plantings (app/controllers/charts/gardens_controller.rb)
Manages scientific naming and taxonomic data integration (app/models/scientific_name.rb)
Handles OAuth authentication for external service connections (app/controllers/authentications_controller.rb)
Implements search functionality across crops, plantings, and activities (app/models/concerns/search_*.rb)
Manages notification system for member communications (app/models/notification.rb)
Provides location-based features with latitude/longitude coordinates (app/models/member.rb location fields)
Implements harvest prediction algorithms based on median values (app/models/concerns/predict_harvest.rb, active_median gem)
Manages alternate crop names in multiple languages (app/models/alternate_name.rb)
Handles garden collaboration and sharing between members (app/models/garden_collaborator.rb)
Provides administrative interfaces for crop wrangling and member management (app/controllers/admin/crops_controller.rb)
use_cases: Home gardeners use this to track their plantings, harvests, and garden activities throughout growing seasons
Urban farmers use this to manage multiple garden spaces and collaborate with other growers
Agricultural researchers access the crowdsourced data via API to study crop performance and growing patterns
Seed savers use this to catalog their seed collections and coordinate seed trades with other members
Garden communities use this to share knowledge, photos, and experiences about food production
analysis.target_audience: Home gardeners, urban farmers, agricultural researchers, seed savers, and gardening communities
api_endpoints: GET /api/v1/crops — List crops with pagination and filtering (swagger/v1/swagger.json)
auth_methods: API token authentication for JSON API (app/controllers/api/v1/base_controller.rb)
integrations: Flickr API for photo management via FlickRaw gem (Gemfile, app/models/concerns/member_flickr.rb)
Mailchimp for newsletter subscriptions via Gibbon gem (Gemfile)
GBIF (Global Biodiversity Information Facility) for taxonomic data (app/models/concerns/gbif_data.rb)

components: Member management system for user accounts and profiles
Garden management system for tracking growing spaces
Crop database with scientific taxonomy and growing information
Planting tracker for individual plant instances
Harvest recorder for yield data collection
Activity planner for garden tasks and scheduling
Photo management system with Flickr integration
Forum and messaging system for community interaction
Admin dashboard for content moderation and user management
JSON API layer for external data access
architecture.data_flow: Members create gardens and plant crops, track activities and harvests over time, upload photos, and share data through the community forum. All data flows through ActiveRecord models to PostgreSQL storage, with search handled by Elasticsearch and photos optionally stored on Flickr. The JSON API exposes aggregated data for external consumption.
architecture.persistence: PostgreSQL primary database with ActiveRecord ORM, 171+ migration files for schema evolution, Elasticsearch for search indexing, optional Flickr for photo storage
architecture.concurrency_model: Traditional Rails threading model with Unicorn HTTP server (Gemfile)
architecture.communication_patterns: HTTP REST for web interface, JSON:API for external consumption, WebSocket not implemented
architecture.plugin_extension_model: Rails engine architecture with Comfortable Mexican Sofa CMS integration (Gemfile)

primary_entities: Member (users with profiles and authentication)
Garden (growing spaces with location data)
Crop (plant species with taxonomic and growing information)
Planting (individual plant instances with dates and status)
Harvest (yield records with quantities and plant parts)
Seed (variety tracking and trading information)
Photo (images associated with crops, plantings, harvests)
Activity (garden tasks with scheduling and completion tracking)
Post (forum discussions and community content)
Comment (discussions on photos and posts)
data_model.schema_patterns: Relational database design with ActiveRecord associations, polymorphic associations for photos and comments, role-based permissions
data_model.migration_strategy: Rails ActiveRecord migrations with 171+ migration files tracking schema evolution from 2012 (db/migrate/)

algorithms.problem_type: Agricultural data collection, aggregation, and analysis for crowdsourced gardening information
core_patterns: Observer pattern for notifications and callbacks
Strategy pattern for different crop data sources (GBIF, OpenFarm)
Factory pattern for test data generation (spec/factories/)
Concern pattern for shared model behavior (app/models/concerns/)
algorithms.complexity_indicators: Medium complexity with 615 Ruby files, comprehensive test suite (277 test files), role-based authorization, and multi-tenant garden collaboration
key_algorithms: Median-based harvest prediction algorithms (active_median gem, app/models/concerns/predict_harvest.rb)
Geographic data handling with latitude/longitude coordinates
algorithms.evidence: app/models/concerns/predict_harvest.rb implements prediction algorithms, app/models/concerns/search_*.rb implement search functionality

security_model.authentication: Devise gem for email/password authentication with OAuth support for external services
security_model.authorization: CanCanCan role-based permissions with admin roles for content moderation (app/models/ability.rb)
security_model.data_protection: Rails standard CSRF protection, encrypted credentials, secure password hashing via Devise
security_model.security_headers: Standard Rails security headers, CSRF protection enabled in ApplicationController
security_model.vulnerability_handling: Dependabot automated dependency updates visible in git history, security contact email in README

deployment.ci_cd: GitHub Actions (visible from CI badge in README)
deployment.environment_config: Environment variables for database, Elasticsearch, Flickr keys, domain configuration (.devcontainer/docker-compose.yml)
deployment.scaling_model: Unicorn HTTP server supports process-based scaling, PostgreSQL and Elasticsearch can be scaled independently

code_quality_signals.test_framework: RSpec (inferred from spec/ directory structure and CONTRIBUTING.md)
code_quality_signals.code_style: Overcommit with multiple linters (rubocop, eslint, coffeelint, csslint per CONTRIBUTING.md and package.json)

project_health.recent_activity: Recent Dependabot update for rubocop suggests ongoing maintenance and security updates
project_health.community_signals: Code of conduct, security policy, contributing guidelines, contributor list, comprehensive wiki documentation

information_gaps: Exact number of git commits (extraction shows only 1 recent commit)
Production deployment configuration beyond development containers
Specific Elasticsearch mapping and indexing strategies

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: System continuously collects crowdsourced data from distributed members, aggregates it into a centralized repository, and provides both API access and analytical views. The core value is transforming individual data points into collective intelligence through structured aggregation patterns.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: Evidence: Comprehensive ActiveRecord schema with 171+ migrations, JSON:API v1 specification (swagger/v1/swagger.json), strong relational database design with foreign key constraints and polymorphic associations
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: Evidence from ActiveRecord ORM patterns, RESTful API endpoints for create/read/update/delete operations on primary entities, median-based harvest prediction algorithms (active_median gem), and Elasticsearch search indexing
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Evidence from PostgreSQL primary database choice, Rails ActiveRecord ORM with transaction support, role-based authorization requiring consistent permission checks
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Maintains a crowdsourced repository where distributed contributors submit structured temporal data about entities they manage, with the system aggregating this data to provide collective insights and enabling collaborative tracking of multi-stage processes over time.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: User input → validation → entity persistence → relationship linking → aggregation → API/UI materialization with optional external service synchronization
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: The core data aggregation and community management patterns are highly reusable. The Rails architecture, role-based permissions, API layer, and crowdsourced data collection framework could easily adapt to other domains. Domain-specific elements are primarily in the crop/garden models and business logic, while the underlying member management, photo handling, messaging, and data export capabilities are generic. The temporal tracking and collaborative features make it valuable for any field requiring community-driven data collection.

evidence_claims.claim: System implements comprehensive JSON:API specification for external data access
evidence_claims.evidence: swagger/v1/swagger.json contains full API specification with endpoints for crops, gardens, plantings, harvests, seeds, activities

evidence_claims.claim: Uses role-based authorization with admin functions for content moderation
evidence_claims.evidence: app/models/ability.rb implements CanCanCan permissions, app/controllers/admin/ directory contains admin-only controllers

evidence_claims.claim: Implements temporal tracking with harvest prediction algorithms
evidence_claims.evidence: app/models/concerns/predict_harvest.rb uses active_median gem for prediction algorithms

evidence_claims.claim: Supports external service integration for photo management
evidence_claims.evidence: app/models/concerns/member_flickr.rb and FlickRaw gem in Gemfile for Flickr API integration

cross_industry_applications: Fitness/Health tracking - members tracking workouts, exercises, and health metrics over time
Hobbyist collecting - tracking collectibles, acquisitions, trades, and community knowledge sharing
Educational research - students and researchers contributing data to collaborative studies
Environmental monitoring - citizen scientists tracking local environmental conditions
Recipe/cooking communities - tracking ingredients, cooking experiments, and sharing results
Pet care tracking - monitoring pet health, feeding, activities with community support
Home maintenance - tracking repairs, improvements, costs with neighborhood collaboration
Investment tracking - portfolio management with community insights and data sharing

complexity_factors: Complex role-based permission system requires business rule changes
Extensive test suite (277 files) needs updating for new domain
External API integrations may need replacement
Domain-specific models and validations throughout codebase
friction_report.migration_path: Clone repository, replace domain models (crop/garden/planting) with target entities, update database schema, modify business logic in controllers, adapt admin interfaces, update API documentation, and replace external integrations as needed

comparable_systems: Strava (fitness tracking with community data aggregation)
Goodreads (book tracking with crowdsourced reviews and recommendations)
Untappd (beer tracking with user-generated venue and product data)
information_gaps: Production deployment architecture and scaling patterns
Specific Elasticsearch indexing and search ranking strategies
Real-world performance metrics and user engagement patterns
Mobile application integration or responsive design details
